text: The endoscopic view reveals a near-circumferential duodenal laterally spreading lesion involving the major papilla, extending approximately six centimeters longitudinally.

The surgeon uses a Carlock needle with the duodenoscope to perform sequential submucosal injections beneath the laterally spreading lesion. The injected solution contains succinylated gelatin, indigo carmine, and adrenaline diluted to one in a hundred thousand.

The surgeon uses a 15mm oval snare with a 0.47mm thickness wire to perform sequential mucosal resection of the laterally spreading lesion (LSL) component. The procedure employs standard EMR polypectomy settings on the ERBI electro generator, with endocut Q effect three, cut duration one, and cut interval six.

The surgeon uses the edge of a previous resection defect as an anchor for the snare, ensuring controlled capture of the tissue. The mobility of the captured tissue is assessed relative to adjacent tissue, confirming no involvement of the muscularis propria and preventing deep mural injury before proceeding with electrocautery resection.
The surgeon performs sequential endoscopic mucosal resection of the laterally spreading lesion using a duodenoscope, carefully removing sections of the mucosa in a controlled manner.

Following a resection, bleeding is observed from a transected arteriole. The surgeon irrigates the area with a triple lumen synchrotome to reveal the source. A coagulation grasper is used to capture the arteriole, and bleeding ceases as the vessel is obliterated with soft coagulating current at 80 watts, effect four, while the forceps are tented away from the mucosa.

The surgeon completes the sequential endoscopic mucosal resection of the laterally spreading component, leaving the ampullary adenoma. The adenoma is captured en bloc with a snare, which is fully tightened, and transection is performed using the specified EMR settings.

A five French single pigtail plastic pancreatic stent is placed prophylactically, followed by the placement of a ten millimeter by six centimeter fully covered metal biliary stent to prevent microperforation and bleeding.
